mentions.note: Great company and surprised to see so low but market prefers Quanta; nothing can change investor perception despite fundamentals.
Down 30% Q1 (65% off all-time high, 10x earnings); oscillates between 'dead from AI' and 'great at AI'—reality: users no longer need subscriptions; Figma/Canva cheaper; can always get cheaper.
Data center critical customer; okay showing today but rally needed more momentum.
Independent oil/gas up 75% Q1 (5th best S&P performer); major producer with zero Persian Gulf exposure—benefits from war supply shock; strong play on oil strength.
Down 41% Q1; second-worst performer; entered year at 45x earnings making stock vulnerable; mobile game growth platform hit by valuation + AI displacement fears.
Unchanged despite down 18% YTD; expected more strength in financial sector rally.
One of 2026's big stocks; refreshed and ready; can run much higher from current levels.
Barely up; Iran war didn't boost financials as expected.
Civil infrastructure software; should work theoretically but market convinced any software company can be disintermediated by AI—won't let it go up.
Fertilizer inputs producer up 68% Q1; bull market in agriculture + Persian Gulf fertilizer shortage; terrific for CF but bad for food prices; at least stick with it.
Fiber optics up 66% Q1; returned to S&P 500 in February; wins as data center buildout demands networking capacity.
Data center fiber optic networking; Nvidia's $2B investment validates thesis; supplies essential data center plumbing.
Data center builder barely held above water today; needs strong rally support.
Down despite rally; retail weakness disappointing.
CEO George Kurtz confirmed cybersecurity NOT vulnerable to AI displacement—actually benefiting; own for charitable trust.
Down 40% Q1; commercial real estate data aggregator easily duplicated by big AI platforms—AI displacement fears validated.
Down 34% Q1 on AI marketplace displacement fears; now cheapest ever; network effects defensible—once user base established, hard to topple; can buy here.
Sole home builder showing strength; rate-cut thesis needs more sector support.
Polyethylene/chemical play up 78% Q1; textbook cyclical rebounded Jan on rate-cut hopes; vulnerable to pullback if strait reopens and oil falls.
Data center buildout critical for AI; okay showing today but rally lacked follow-through.
Up in rally; sole bright spot in home retail.
Buying back Irish facility sold to PE during crisis—sign of balance sheet strength; now rock solid after long teetering.
Down 35% Q1 but +8% since CEO Sasan Goodarzi spoke Feb 26; brand loyalty, consumer trust, expert network moat vs. AI replication; deserves comeback but needs time as market written it off.
Barely up despite expected rate-cut driven strength; financial sector disappointing.
Fiber optics play up 90.7% Q1; Nvidia invested $2B cementing relationship; wins as long as AI data center buildout continues unabated.

Tirzepatide pill approved—game-changing convenience vs. Novo; acquired Synaptic for $7.8B targeting narcolepsy/neurological disorders via orexin; weight-loss revenue wisely deployed; own for charitable trust.
Down despite rally; home retail weakness.
Petrochemical up 86% Q1 on Iran strait closure supply shock; already bouncing Jan before war on rate-cut hopes; at risk of pullback if war ends and strait reopens.
Data center buildout essential; okay showing today but rally needed broader leadership.
Up 72% Q1 (6th best S&P); pandemic cleanup after wilderness years; new flu vaccine FDA review, settled lawsuits, RFK Jr.'s vaccine-skeptic departure huge catalysts; momentum building.
Dominates Windows with captive office audience and mandatory Copilot pushes yet stock can't move higher—palpable sense something very wrong; on borrowed time in charitable trust.
Only company adding capacity at scale but still sold out; Q1 earnings excellent but stock fell 30% mid-March—now rebounding with war talks; true growth story vs. peers.
Gold miner leading gainers list is letdown; not best-in-class (Newmont > Agnico Eagle); symbol of narrow rally lacking growth stocks.
Started strong today but fizzled; deserves premium multiple as actual producer of desired chips but rally needs real leadership from mega-cap.
Higher-risk crude play up 58% Q1; big winner when oil rallies, big loser when it falls—depends on Iran keeping strait closed for continued gains.
CEO Nikesh Arora confirmed cybersecurity benefiting from AI not disrupted; preferred over Zscaler; own for charitable trust.
Great company rebuilding base after overheating; strong business, customers love product; expect great 2026-27 despite recent pullback.
Down 27 points (19%) after bad quarter; 2.4B debt, missed estimates, bought back stock at higher prices—suboptimal situation.
Down 26% Q1; great business with growth/profitability but 64x earnings unjustifiable; no AI disruption case but money managers won't pay multiple for high-growth SaaS anymore.
Recommended as short for many years; activist investor may force action/sale but nothing interesting at current levels despite memory monetization updates.
Flash memory storage up 167.7% Q1; shortage-driven rally resilient but historic cycles suggest 6 quarters typical—already at peak gains.
Storage manufacturer riding data center memory shortage; new capacity not until 2028-29; benefiting from price-raising advantage.
Down 58% Q1—worst Nasdaq 100 performer; makes collaboration software for developers but AI now writes code—ground zero for displacement fears despite solid February quarter.
Semiconductor test and measurement equipment; narrow play on chipmaker capacity expansion.
Down in rally; retail complex showing weakness.
Permian land owner up 65% Q1 (9th best); leases surface/sells fracking water; down 14% this week anticipating war end and oil decline—prelude to pullback if strait reopens.
Down 32% Q1; financial data + Westlaw legal research easy targets for AI competition but no rush to abandon while Claude unproven—won't wait to find out.
Late in silver rally game; own Franco Eagle (FEO) instead; VZLA too risky as Vancouver junior.
Down 40% Q1; double-whammy: enterprise software + HCM focus vulnerable to AI-driven layoffs; CEO stepped down Jan/Feb, mixed quarter—don't try to be hero in this group.
Memory storage plays benefiting from data center shortage; sold out, can raise prices but only 4 quarters of boom cycle remain—easy money already made.
Barely up in rally; pathetic showing for retail strength needed to validate broad advance.
Down 38% Q1 on cybersecurity displacement fears wrongly applied; CrowdStrike/Palo Alto prove security actually gaining from AI—not vulnerable; prefer CrowdStrike/Palo Alto over Zscaler though.
